summary: Symbol-level pyfly parity sweep across all 41 packages + admin + tiers + DI → complete gap report
logs: exhaustive audit: 14 groups over 41 pyfly packages

groups.group: kernel, config, context, core, domain, validation, i18n
container (DI) — pyfly container + DI-adjacent context decorators vs firefly-container + firefly-macros

gaps.symbol: @conditional_on_property / @conditional_on_class / @conditional_on_missing_bean / @conditional_on_bean / @conditional_on_single_candidate / @conditional_on_web_application / @conditional_on_resource / @conditional_on_expression / @auto_configuration
gaps.pyfly_ref: src/pyfly/context/conditions.py (all exported from pyfly.context.__init__) — Spring-Boot @ConditionalOn* family, evaluated at ApplicationContext startup against Environment/config/registry
gaps.rust_state: absent — firefly-macros #[derive(Component/Service/Repository)] (crates/macros/src/container.rs) supports only scope/name/primary; firefly-container (crates/container/src/lib.rs) has primary/order/named resolution but NO conditional gating; no context-level condition evaluator. firefly-config has accepts_profiles()/active_profiles() but nothing wires it to bean registration.
gaps.recommendation: Add a #[firefly(condition = ...)] attribute family (or a builder API on Container) that gates registration on: config property present/equal (on_property), feature/type availability (on_class→cfg or registry probe), bean present/absent (on_bean/on_missing_bean/on_single_candidate), and active profile. The generated firefly_register should consult Environment/Container before inserting. This is the central DI-parity item already scoped for W2.
gaps.group: kernel, config, context, core, domain, validation, i18n

gaps.symbol: @config_properties + Config.bind() + @Value descriptor
gaps.pyfly_ref: src/pyfly/core/config.py (config_properties, Config.bind relaxed dataclass/Pydantic binding) and src/pyfly/core/value.py (Value descriptor resolving ${key}/${key:default}/#{expr} at bean creation)
gaps.rust_state: partial — firefly-config load()/bind() (crates/config/src/binder.rs) covers typed relaxed binding + placeholders, so @config_properties' binding half is covered. BUT there is no DI integration: a bound config struct is not auto-registered as an injectable bean, and there is no per-field @Value-style config injection on components (the #[derive(Component)] field attrs are only #[autowired] for bean injection or Default).
gaps.recommendation: Add a #[firefly(value = "${key:default}")] field attribute on #[derive(Component)] that resolves against a Config/Environment at construction, and a way to register a bound @config_properties struct as a resolvable bean (e.g. #[derive(ConfigProperties)] generating a factory that calls firefly_config::bind). Mirrors Spring @EnableConfigurationProperties + @Value.
gaps.group: kernel, config, context, core, domain, validation, i18n

gaps.symbol: SpEL-lite expression evaluator (pyfly.core.expression: is_expression/evaluate, #{...} grammar)
gaps.pyfly_ref: src/pyfly/core/expression.py — safe AST-based evaluator for #{...} (arithmetic/comparison/boolean/ternary, ${key:default} substitution, env map); consumed by @Value and @conditional_on_expression
gaps.rust_state: absent — no #{...} expression support anywhere; firefly-config only resolves ${...} placeholders (crates/config/src/placeholder.rs). accepts_profiles() handles profile boolean grammar only.
gaps.recommendation: Provide a small const/dynamic expression evaluator (or document that #{...} reduces to ${...} + Rust code) so @conditional_on_expression and @Value expression forms have a counterpart. Lower priority than the conditional/value attributes themselves; could be deferred or scoped to simple boolean/comparison forms used by conditionals.
gaps.group: kernel, config, context, core, domain, validation, i18n

gaps.symbol: MessageSource port + positional MessageFormat substitution + ResourceBundleMessageSource + FixedLocaleResolver/LocaleResolver port
gaps.pyfly_ref: src/pyfly/i18n/ports/outbound.py (MessageSource Protocol: get_message/get_message_or_default raising KeyError), src/pyfly/i18n/adapters/resource_bundle.py (loads messages_{locale}.yaml|yml|json by convention, flattens nested keys, java.text.MessageFormat {0}/{1} positional args with quote semantics), src/pyfly/i18n/locale.py (LocaleResolver Protocol, FixedLocaleResolver, AcceptHeaderLocaleResolver)
gaps.rust_state: partial — firefly-i18n (crates/i18n/src/lib.rs) Bundle::t + LocaleLayer cover translate + Accept-Language resolution, and load_json/load_yaml exist. Gaps: (1) substitution is NAMED {name} only, not positional {0}/{1} MessageFormat with quoting — pyfly users passing tuple args get no parity; (2) no pluggable MessageSource trait/port (Bundle is concrete) and no get_message_or_default/KeyError-on-miss semantics; (3) no ResourceBundleMessageSource-style on-disk messages_{locale}.* directory loader (load_json/load_yaml take content strings, not a base path/locale convention); (4) no FixedLocaleResolver / LocaleResolver port abstraction (only the Accept-Language layer).
gaps.recommendation: Add a MessageSource trait (get_message / get_message_or_default), a positional {0}/{1} MessageFormat formatter alongside the named one (or a feature flag), a file-convention loader (load_dir(base, locale) reading messages_{locale}.yaml|json with key flattening), and a LocaleResolver trait with Fixed + AcceptHeader implementations so a migrating pyfly user has the same pluggable surface.
gaps.group: kernel, config, context, core, domain, validation, i18n

gaps.symbol: RefreshScopeRefreshedEvent
gaps.pyfly_ref: src/pyfly/context/events.py (RefreshScopeRefreshedEvent, exported from pyfly.context) — published by ContextRefresher.refresh() carrying the evicted refresh-scoped bean keys; also pyfly.context.refresh.ContextRefresher
gaps.rust_state: absent — firefly-config exposes ContextRefreshedEvent/ApplicationReadyEvent/ContextClosedEvent and the Refresher trait/ReloadableConfig (crates/config/src/{events,reload}.rs), and firefly-container has RefreshScope. But there is no RefreshScopeRefreshedEvent type, and nothing publishes a refresh-completed event listing evicted keys when RefreshScope is refreshed.
gaps.recommendation: Add a RefreshScopeRefreshedEvent { refreshed: Vec<String> } to firefly-config events and publish it from the refresh path (Refresher/RefreshScope integration) so listeners can react to a Spring-Cloud-style /actuator/refresh, matching pyfly.
gaps.group: kernel, config, context, core, domain, validation, i18n

gaps.symbol: @post_construct / @pre_destroy lifecycle callbacks + BeanPostProcessor
gaps.pyfly_ref: src/pyfly/context/lifecycle.py (post_construct/pre_destroy) and src/pyfly/context/post_processor.py (BeanPostProcessor before_init/after_init), both exported from pyfly.context
gaps.rust_state: absent at the container level — firefly-container (crates/container/src/lib.rs) has scopes but no init/destroy callback or post-processor hook on bean creation. The firefly-lifecycle Application provides app-level on_start/on_stop hooks (the established idiom for adapter start/stop, covering kernel.Lifecycle), but there is no per-bean @post_construct/@pre_destroy nor a BeanPostProcessor SPI invoked around each resolve.
gaps.recommendation: Largely an established-idiom adaptation (Rust uses Default/explicit constructors + Drop + Application hooks), so not a hard blocker. If full Spring parity is wanted, add an optional post-processor SPI on Container (run a closure before/after a factory produces a bean) and an init/destroy hook on Registration. Lowest priority — most migrating users will use the lifecycle Application hooks.
gaps.group: kernel, config, context, core, domain, validation, i18n

gaps.symbol: Automatic OpenAPI generation from controllers (OpenAPIGenerator driven by RouteMetadata)
gaps.pyfly_ref: src/pyfly/web/openapi.py (OpenAPIGenerator); src/pyfly/web/adapters/starlette/controller.py (ControllerRegistrar.collect_route_metadata) + app.py wiring /openapi.json,/docs,/redoc
gaps.rust_state: partial — firefly-openapi (crates/openapi/src/lib.rs) generates OpenAPI 3.1 + serves /openapi.json and a Swagger-UI shim at /openapi/ui, but ONLY from hand-registered RouteDef + serde_json Sample values. The #[rest_controller] macro (crates/macros/src/web.rs) emits only a routes()->axum::Router fn; it produces NO RouteDef/route-metadata, so paths, params (PathVar/QueryParam/Header/Cookie), request-body model, response schema, per-class tag, and docstring summary/description are NOT auto-derived. No ReDoc endpoint.
gaps.recommendation: Have #[rest_controller] additionally emit RouteDef descriptors (method, joined path, tags from controller type name) and a routes_openapi()/register-with-Builder hook so firefly-openapi can build the spec without the user re-declaring every route. Optionally derive request/response schemas from the handler's Json<T>/extractor types (via a #[derive(JsonSchema)]-style or serde Sample). Add a ReDoc HTML endpoint to match pyfly's /redoc.

gaps.symbol: @controller_advice + @exception_handler (controller-local and global exception-to-response mapping)
gaps.pyfly_ref: src/pyfly/container/stereotypes.controller_advice; src/pyfly/web/exception_handler.py; controller.py (_collect_exception_handlers / _collect_global_advice_handlers, MRO-ordered, controller-local overrides global)
gaps.rust_state: absent as a customization hook — errors are rendered uniformly by firefly_web::ProblemLayer/WebError (crates/web/src/problem.rs) from FireflyError's status, which covers the default RFC 7807 path. There is no mechanism to register a per-controller or global handler that maps a specific error/exception type to a custom status+body, nor controller-local-overrides-global precedence.
gaps.recommendation: Provide an ergonomic error-mapping hook: e.g. an ExceptionHandlerRegistry / a per-controller handler trait, or document the idiomatic Rust pattern (custom IntoResponse / From<MyError> for WebError) as the controller_advice equivalent. At minimum expose a global error-mapping layer so a migrating user can translate domain errors to custom responses without hand-writing IntoResponse per type.

gaps.symbol: Global JSON config: JsonProperties (pyfly.web.json.* — property-naming-strategy camelCase, by-alias, exclude-none, exclude-defaults, fail-on-unknown-properties) + CamelModel + JsonSerializers registry
gaps.pyfly_ref: src/pyfly/web/json.py (JsonProperties, CamelModel, JsonSerializers, PyFlyJsonSerializer, json_properties_from_config); message_converters.install_serialization_state wires it onto app.state
gaps.rust_state: partial — crates/web/src/content_negotiation.rs JsonMessageConverter is stateless serde_json with no config knobs. camelCase/exclude-none are reachable per-type via serde attributes (idiomatic), but there is NO global pyfly.web.json.* switch, NO fail_on_unknown_properties enforcement at the read boundary (serde is lenient by default; needs #[serde(deny_unknown_fields)] per type), and NO JsonSerializers registry to centrally encode arbitrary non-serde types.
gaps.recommendation: Add a configurable JsonMessageConverter (or serialization-state struct on the router/state) bound from config: camelCase naming, exclude-none/defaults, and a deny-unknown-fields toggle applied at deserialize. For fail-on-unknown, wire serde_json's error path or a strict-mode wrapper. Document the serde-attribute equivalents for the per-type cases so migrating users know the mapping.

gaps.symbol: @deprecated endpoint marker -> OpenAPI deprecated:true
gaps.pyfly_ref: src/pyfly/web/mappings.py:deprecated; openapi.py sets operation['deprecated']; controller.py reads __pyfly_deprecated__
gaps.rust_state: absent — neither the #[rest_controller] verb macros (crates/macros/src/web.rs) nor firefly-openapi RouteDef/Operation (crates/openapi/src/lib.rs) carry a deprecated flag.
gaps.recommendation: Add a `deprecated` field to RouteDef/Operation in firefly-openapi and a way to set it from the controller macro (e.g. #[deprecated] passthrough or a mapping attribute), so deprecated endpoints render with deprecated:true in the spec.

gaps.symbol: DI-based controller auto-discovery + route mounting (scan @rest_controller beans and mount automatically)
gaps.pyfly_ref: src/pyfly/web/adapters/starlette/app.py create_app + controller.ControllerRegistrar.collect_routes (auto-mounts all controller beans from the ApplicationContext, incl. @bean-produced ones via post-start rescan)
gaps.rust_state: absent by design — crates/container/src/lib.rs documents stereotype scanning as Python-runtime-only; Rust requires explicit .merge(MyController::routes(state)). This is the established Rust idiom (excluded from gap criteria), noted for completeness, not as a defect.
gaps.recommendation: No action required for parity. Optionally provide an inventory/linkme-based opt-in route-collection helper if zero-wiring discovery becomes a desired ergonomic, but explicit mounting is acceptable and idiomatic.

gaps.symbol: CQRS domain-event publishing pipeline: @publish_domain_event decorator, CommandEventPublisher / EdaCommandEventPublisher / NoOpEventPublisher, EventFailureStrategy (LOG|RAISE), and the command-bus step that harvests result.domain_events / command.domain_events and forwards each to EDA after a successful command
gaps.pyfly_ref: src/pyfly/cqrs/event/decorators.py (publish_domain_event), src/pyfly/cqrs/event/publisher.py (CommandEventPublisher, EdaCommandEventPublisher, NoOpEventPublisher), src/pyfly/cqrs/command/bus.py (EventFailureStrategy, DefaultCommandBus._try_publish_events), config/auto_configuration.py (command_event_publisher bean)
gaps.rust_state: absent — crates/cqrs has no event-publishing concept. The Bus (crates/cqrs/src/bus.rs) dispatches a handler and returns the typed result; there is no post-handler hook that collects domain events and pushes them to firefly_eda. The reverse direction exists (crates/cqrs/src/eda_bridge.rs consumes EDA events to invalidate the cache), but emitting domain events from a command handler is not modelled. grep for domain_events/EventPublisher/CommandEventPublisher in crates/cqrs returns nothing.
gaps.recommendation: Add an outbound EDA bridge to crates/cqrs: a CommandEventPublisher port plus an EdaCommandEventPublisher over firefly_eda::Publisher, a way to attach emitted events to a dispatch (e.g. a trait the handler result implements exposing domain_events, or middleware reading an envelope side-channel), an EventFailureStrategy { Log, Raise } knob, and a #[command_handler(destination = "...")] arg mapping to pyfly's @publish_domain_event(destination=...). A pyfly user wiring EdaCommandEventPublisher today has no Rust counterpart and must hand-roll event emission inside every handler.

gaps.symbol: CqrsMetricsService — command/query processed, failed, validation-failed counters and processing-time histograms (firefly.cqrs.command.processed / .failed / .validation.failed / .processing.time and the query equivalents)
gaps.pyfly_ref: src/pyfly/cqrs/command/metrics.py (CqrsMetricsService), wired into DefaultCommandBus / DefaultQueryBus pipelines and config/auto_configuration.py (cqrs_metrics_service bean)
gaps.rust_state: absent — there is no metrics middleware or metrics service in crates/cqrs (grep for metrics/counter/histogram/MetricsRegistry in crates/cqrs/src returns nothing). The Bus runs validation/authorization/cache middleware but records no per-dispatch metrics. crates/observability has a metrics registry, but nothing connects CQRS dispatch to it.
gaps.recommendation: Add a MetricsMiddleware (or a CqrsMetrics helper) in crates/cqrs that records success/failure counters and a processing-time histogram per message type, using the same metric names as pyfly (firefly_cqrs_command_processed etc.) so dashboards port across runtimes. Bridge it to the firefly_observability metrics registry. Without it, migrating pyfly services lose CQRS throughput/latency observability.

gaps.symbol: CommandHandler / QueryHandler template-method lifecycle hooks: pre_process, post_process, on_success, on_error, map_error (and the do_handle_with_context / ContextAware* split)
gaps.pyfly_ref: src/pyfly/cqrs/command/handler.py and src/pyfly/cqrs/query/handler.py (handle() template method invoking pre_process → do_handle → post_process → on_success, with on_error + map_error on the failure path)
gaps.rust_state: absent — Rust handlers are plain async fns/closures registered via Bus::register / register_with_context (crates/cqrs/src/bus.rs); there is no handler base type and no per-handler lifecycle hooks. The #[command_handler]/#[query_handler] macros (crates/macros/src/cqrs.rs) wrap a bare async fn with no pre/post/error hooks. Cross-cutting concerns can be expressed as bus Middleware, but per-handler enrichment/error-mapping (map_error) has no direct analog.
gaps.recommendation: Document the Middleware path as the idiom for pre/post/on_error, and consider an optional handler-error-mapping hook (pyfly's map_error) — e.g. a per-registration error mapper or a trait. If treated as covered-by-middleware, at minimum add a migration note; map_error specifically has no middleware-equivalent today because middleware cannot rewrite a handler error into a domain-specific exception keyed to the handler.

gaps.symbol: Handler decorator operational metadata: @command_handler/@query_handler kwargs timeout, retries, backoff_ms, priority, tags, description, metrics, tracing, validation (and query-side cache_key_prefix)
gaps.pyfly_ref: src/pyfly/cqrs/decorators.py (command_handler / query_handler overloads setting __pyfly_timeout__, __pyfly_retries__, __pyfly_backoff_ms__, __pyfly_priority__, __pyfly_tags__, __pyfly_description__, __pyfly_metrics__, __pyfly_tracing__, __pyfly_validation__, __pyfly_cache_key_prefix__)
gaps.rust_state: partial — the Rust #[command_handler]/#[query_handler] attribute macros (crates/macros/src/cqrs.rs) accept only `crate` and `register` (registration-fn name) args. Cacheability/TTL is covered separately via #[derive(Query)] #[firefly(cache_ttl = "...")] → Message::cache_ttl. The remaining operational knobs (timeout, retries, backoff, priority, tags, description, per-handler metrics/tracing/validation toggles) are not expressible.
gaps.recommendation: Either (a) add accepted args to the handler macros that wire to existing mechanisms — e.g. timeout/retries/backoff via firefly_resilience decorators around the registered closure — or (b) explicitly document these as out-of-scope/handled-by-resilience-middleware. timeout+retries+backoff are the user-visible ones a pyfly handler author will look for; tags/priority/description are informational only.

gaps.symbol: CqrsHealthIndicator — actuator health contributor reporting UP/UNKNOWN based on registered handler count
gaps.pyfly_ref: src/pyfly/cqrs/actuator/health.py (CqrsHealthIndicator.health() → HealthStatus UP when handlers>0 else UNKNOWN), config/auto_configuration.py (cqrs_health_indicator bean contributing to /actuator/health)
gaps.rust_state: partial — the admin actuator exposes a CQRS handler listing at /admin/api/cqrs via Bus::handler_names (crates/admin/src/data.rs cqrs(), crates/admin/src/router.rs), which covers pyfly's CqrsMetricsEndpoint, but there is no CQRS HealthIndicator feeding the composite /admin/api/health. So the handler inventory is surfaced, but CQRS does not contribute an UP/UNKNOWN status to the health composite.
gaps.recommendation: Add a CQRS health contributor that reports UP when the Bus has at least one registered handler (else UNKNOWN), wired into the firefly_observability/admin health composite, mirroring pyfly's CqrsHealthIndicator. Low impact since the handler listing already exposes the same underlying signal.

gaps.symbol: StoredEventEnvelope wire fields & (de)serialization: distinct event_id + sequence (separate from version) + aggregate_type-per-event + metadata column + occurred_at, plus to_json()/from_json() helpers and StoredEventEnvelope.of(...)
gaps.pyfly_ref: src/pyfly/eventsourcing/event.py (StoredEventEnvelope with event_id, aggregate_id, aggregate_type, sequence, event_type, payload, metadata, occurred_at, version, tenant_id; to_json/from_json/of) and store.py (SqlAlchemyEventStore persists all of these columns)
gaps.rust_state: partial — crates/eventsourcing models a leaner DomainEvent (aggregate_id, aggregate_type, version, event_type, time, payload, metadata, tenant_id) plus a StreamedEvent { event_id, event } wrapper for the global stream. The Rust SQL store DDL (crates/eventsourcing/src/sql_store.rs) folds sequence into `version` (UNIQUE(aggregate_id, version)) and does NOT persist a separate `sequence`, an event-level `aggregate_type` column, or a `metadata` column (metadata rides inside the payload/JSON). Functionally equivalent for single-stream ordering, but the wire/storage shape diverges from pyfly's envelope and there are no to_json/from_json envelope helpers.
gaps.recommendation: If cross-runtime event-store interop with pyfly producers/consumers is a goal, align the SQL schema/wire shape (add sequence + aggregate_type + metadata columns, or document the mapping version==sequence) and consider envelope JSON helpers. If interop is not required, document the intentional divergence. Lower priority than the CQRS gaps because the in-process semantics (ordering, optimistic concurrency, replay, snapshots, upcasting, outbox) are all present and correct.

gaps.symbol: Orchestration observability layer: OrchestrationEvents (Protocol/listener), CompositeOrchestrationEvents, LoggerOrchestrationEvents, OrchestrationMetrics, OrchestrationTracer
gaps.pyfly_ref: pyfly.transactional top-level __init__.py exports all five; src/pyfly/transactional/core/events.py (OrchestrationEvents protocol with ~25 lifecycle hooks: on_start/on_completed/on_step_started/on_step_success/on_step_failed/on_step_skipped/on_compensation_started/on_step_compensated/on_phase_started/on_phase_completed/on_phase_failed/on_participant_*/on_workflow_suspended/on_workflow_resumed/on_signal_delivered/on_timer_fired/on_child_workflow_*/on_continue_as_new/on_dead_lettered), core/metrics.py (OrchestrationMetrics: per-name started/completed/failed counters, execution_duration + step_latency histograms with p50/p95, compensations, dead_letters, tcc_phases), core/tracer.py (OrchestrationTracer span/is_enabled)
gaps.rust_state: absent — grep across all 72 crates returns zero hits for OrchestrationEvents/OrchestrationMetrics/OrchestrationTracer or any on_step_started/on_phase_started/on_dead_lettered emission. firefly-orchestration engines (saga.rs/workflow.rs/tcc.rs) have no listener hook, no metrics recording, no tracing span emission, and no outbound per-step/workflow event publishing (pyfly @step_event / output_event_type / publish_events). The generic firefly-actuator/observability crates are not wired to the engines.
gaps.recommendation: Add an observability module to firefly-orchestration: (1) an OrchestrationEvents listener trait (async-trait) with the full pyfly hook set, plus CompositeOrchestrationEvents (fan-out) and a tracing-backed LoggerOrchestrationEvents default; (2) thread an Arc<dyn OrchestrationEvents> through Saga::run/Workflow::run/Tcc::run so lifecycle hooks (start/step/compensation/phase/participant/suspend/resume/signal/timer/child/continue-as-new/dead-letter) fire; (3) an OrchestrationMetrics implementor recording counters + latency histograms (reuse firefly-observability/Prometheus); (4) optional OpenTelemetry span emission. This is the largest parity gap and a migrating pyfly user wiring metrics/tracing/event-listeners would hit it immediately.

gaps.symbol: Saga composition: SagaComposition, CompositionEntry, SagaDataFlow, SagaCompositor, SagaCompositionBuilder, CompositionContext, CompensationManager, DataFlowManager, CompositionValidator
gaps.pyfly_ref: src/pyfly/transactional/saga/composition/ (8 modules, 9 public symbols via composition/__init__.py): orchestrates MULTIPLE sagas as a DAG — layer-based concurrent execution (asyncio.gather per layer via SagaTopology.compute_layers), declarative inter-saga data flow (SagaDataFlow maps source_saga[.source_step] -> target_key into downstream saga input, resolved by DataFlowManager.resolve_input), cross-saga compensation of all completed sagas on failure (CompensationManager.compensate_completed), and DAG validation (CompositionValidator.validate)
gaps.rust_state: absent — grep for composit/compositor/data_flow/SagaComposition across crates/ finds only unrelated 'composite/decomposes' mentions; no multi-saga composition exists. firefly-orchestration covers single-saga and single-workflow DAGs but not orchestrating several registered sagas together with data wiring + aggregate compensation.
gaps.recommendation: Port the composition subpackage as a new module in firefly-orchestration (e.g. saga_composition.rs): SagaComposition definition + CompositionEntry (depends_on + data_flows) + SagaDataFlow; a SagaCompositor that computes DAG layers, runs same-layer sagas concurrently (tokio::join/JoinSet), resolves downstream input via a DataFlowManager from prior SagaResults, and compensates completed sagas in reverse on failure; plus a SagaCompositionBuilder and CompositionValidator. Reuse the existing OrchestrationValidator topology/DAG primitives.

gaps.symbol: TCC @FromTry — Try-phase result injection into Confirm/Cancel
gaps.pyfly_ref: src/pyfly/transactional/tcc/annotations.py FromTry + core/argument.py resolver (ctx.get_try_result(pid)); confirm/cancel methods declare Annotated[T, FromTry()] to receive the value the try method returned
gaps.rust_state: partial — firefly-orchestration tcc.rs constructs a StepContext internally but invokes try/confirm/cancel as zero-arg ActionFn closures (try_action/confirm_action/cancel_action). There is no TccParticipant::with_context variant and no mechanism for confirm/cancel to read the try phase's result, so the @FromTry data-passing capability is missing (saga/workflow already have with_context + StepContext; TCC does not).
gaps.recommendation: Add context-aware TCC participant builders (e.g. TccParticipant::with_context taking try/confirm/cancel closures that receive &StepContext) and thread the run's StepContext so try can set_result and confirm/cancel can read it — mirroring the existing Step::with_context / Node::with_context pattern. Then confirm/cancel can consume the try outcome, achieving @FromTry parity.

gaps.symbol: Redis / Cache execution-persistence adapters: RedisPersistenceProvider, CachePersistenceProvider
gaps.pyfly_ref: src/pyfly/transactional/persistence/__init__.py exports RedisPersistenceProvider, CachePersistenceProvider, SqlAlchemyPersistenceProvider (concrete ExecutionPersistenceProvider adapters)
gaps.rust_state: partial — firefly-orchestration persistence.rs ships the PersistenceProvider port plus MemoryPersistence and SqlitePersistence (the SQLAlchemy analogue). No Redis-backed or generic-cache-backed adapter exists, despite the workspace having firefly-cache-redis / firefly-session-redis crates to build on. SqlAlchemy parity is met; Redis/cache parity is not.
gaps.recommendation: Provide a Redis-backed PersistenceProvider (key = correlation_id, serialized ExecutionState; secondary indexes for status/pattern/stale scans) and/or a cache-abstraction-backed adapter wired to firefly-cache, so production deployments can persist orchestration state in Redis as pyfly does. Lower urgency since the durable SQLite adapter and the port both exist and any backend can be implemented externally.

gaps.symbol: OrchestrationHealthIndicator
gaps.pyfly_ref: src/pyfly/transactional/health.py — actuator HealthIndicator wrapping ExecutionPersistenceProvider.is_healthy(), reported to /actuator/health (top-level export)
gaps.rust_state: absent (trivially closeable) — no orchestration-specific HealthIndicator; firefly-actuator provides the generic HealthIndicator trait + IndicatorFn closure adapter, and PersistenceProvider::is_healthy() exists, but no convenience adapter ties them together or is registered.
gaps.recommendation: Add a small OrchestrationHealthIndicator in firefly-orchestration implementing firefly_actuator::HealthIndicator over Arc<dyn PersistenceProvider>, returning UP/DOWN from is_healthy() with a {persistence: ok|unreachable} detail, and document registering it with the actuator HealthRegistry.

gaps.symbol: Per-step report timing fields (StepReport.started_at / completed_at / latency_ms)
gaps.pyfly_ref: src/pyfly/transactional/core/report.py StepReport carries started_at, completed_at, latency_ms, attempts, result, error (built from StepRecord via ExecutionReportBuilder)
gaps.rust_state: partial — firefly-orchestration report.rs StepReport has step_id/status/attempts/result/error but omits started_at, completed_at and latency_ms (the saga Outcome it is built from records only aggregate started_at/finished_at, not per-step timings).
gaps.recommendation: Capture per-step start/finish timestamps in the engine (StepContext or a StepRecord-style structure) and surface started_at/completed_at/latency_ms on StepReport so the execution report matches pyfly's per-step latency detail. Minor; only matters for fine-grained report consumers.

gaps.symbol: Derived query methods executed end-to-end (BaseRepositoryPostProcessor + RepositoryBeanPostProcessor / MongoRepositoryBeanPostProcessor wiring method names onto repository beans)
gaps.pyfly_ref: src/pyfly/data/post_processor.py (BaseRepositoryPostProcessor.after_init); src/pyfly/data/relational/sqlalchemy/post_processor.py; src/pyfly/data/document/mongodb/post_processor.py; src/pyfly/data/query_parser.py

gaps.symbol: @lazy bean marker (Spring @Lazy: skip eager singleton init at startup)
gaps.pyfly_ref: src/pyfly/container/lazy.py:23-31 (sets __pyfly_lazy__) consumed by application_context.py:211 (eager-init loop skips lazy singletons)
gaps.rust_state: absent (and partially moot) — no #[lazy] macro option. The Rust container already creates singletons lazily on first resolve (resolve_registration builds on demand), so the runtime behavior is lazy by default. The gap is the eager-startup semantics: pyfly eagerly instantiates non-lazy singletons at context start (failing fast on misconfiguration); Rust has no eager-init pass, so there is no notion to opt out of.
gaps.recommendation: Tie to the ApplicationContext gap: if/when an eager-singleton-warmup pass is added, expose #[firefly(lazy)] to opt out. Standalone it is low priority because Rust is already lazy; document the behavioral difference so pyfly users don't expect fail-fast-at-boot.
